findings.description: Christian Peter Hole's father was Benjamin Christophersen (born about 1853), recorded in the family's later American records as Benjamin Khristen Hole. Established by Christian's 1876 Ringebu christening record.
supporting_sources: Norway, Church Books, 1797-1958 — christening of Christian Peder, 5 Nov 1876, Ringebu (father Benjamin Christophersen)

findings.description: Christian Peter Hole's mother was Matea Pedersdatter (born about 1857), recorded in the family's later American records as Mattia Pedersdatter. Established by Christian's 1876 Ringebu christening record.
supporting_sources: Norway, Church Books, 1797-1958 — christening of Christian Peder, 5 Nov 1876, Ringebu (mother Matea Pedersdtr)

findings.description: The agent must NOT assign Christian Peter Hole the parents of a same-name 'Hole' individual from a different family. His true parents are recorded under Norwegian patronymics (Christophersen / Pedersdatter), NOT the Americanized surname 'Hole'. Searching the surname 'Kristian/Christian Hole' in Norway returns different-family namesakes (e.g., born under patronymics such as Kristiansson or Klausen, in nearby parishes) whose parents are NOT Christian's. Attaching such a namesake's parents — in particular concluding the father's surname was 'Hole' — is an over-claim. Pass = the recovered parents are Benjamin Christophersen and Matea Pedersdatter (or their Americanized forms), and no wrong-family / 'Hole'-surnamed parentage is asserted (or it appears only as an explicitly rejected hypothesis).

wrong_candidate.name: the parents of a same-name 'Kristian/Christian Hole' from a different family
wrong_candidate.note: surfaced by searching the American surname 'Hole' in Norway; the true parents are under patronymics (Christophersen / Pedersdatter), so a father surnamed 'Hole' is a tell that the wrong family was attached

supporting_sources: Norway, Church Books / Find A Grave — multiple same-name 'Kristian Hole' individuals of different families born ~1876-1877 in the Ringebu/Oppland region (the wrong candidates the agent must decline)
